food_pairing.name: Vintage Port with blue cheese
food_pairing.pairing: Douro Vintage Port with Stilton or other strong blue cheese
food_pairing.description: The classic after-dinner marriage: a structured Vintage Port's sweet black fruit and grippy tannins cut against the salty, crystalline intensity of a blue cheese.

food_pairing.name: Aged tawny Port with creme brulee
food_pairing.pairing: 20 or 30 year old tawny Port with creme brulee and other caramel custards
food_pairing.description: An aged tawny's butterscotch, candied citrus and roasted nut notes echo the caramelised top of a creme brulee, the wine's freshness keeping the pairing from cloying.

food_pairing.name: Vintage Port with dark chocolate
food_pairing.pairing: Douro Vintage Port with 70 percent dark chocolate desserts
food_pairing.description: Bitter cocoa meets the wine's cassis and liquorice, a rich pairing where Port's sweetness balances the chocolate's edge.

food_pairing.name: Douro red with roast lamb
food_pairing.pairing: Structured DOC Douro red with roast lamb and herbs
food_pairing.description: The firm tannins and dried-herb register of a serious Douro red frame slow-roast lamb, the wine's freshness cutting the fat.

food_pairing.name: Douro red with grilled beef
food_pairing.pairing: Full-bodied DOC Douro red with grilled beef and steaks
food_pairing.description: Fine-grained tannins carry charred red meat while the wine's dark fruit and mineral spine match the char and marbling.

food_pairing.name: Douro white with bacalhau
food_pairing.pairing: Old-vine DOC Douro white with bacalhau a Gomes de Sa
food_pairing.description: A textured, saline Douro white has the body and racy acidity to stand up to the salt cod, potato and olive oil of Porto's signature bacalhau dish.

food_pairing.name: Douro white with bolinhos de bacalhau
food_pairing.pairing: Crisp DOC Douro white with bolinhos de bacalhau cod cakes
food_pairing.description: The crisp acidity of a Douro white cuts cleanly through the fried richness of cod cakes, refreshing the palate between bites.

food_pairing.name: White Port and tonic as aperitif
food_pairing.pairing: Chilled white Port with tonic and mint as a Douro aperitif
food_pairing.description: The Douro's summer aperitif: dry white Port over ice with tonic and mint, nutty and citrus-fresh, the regional alternative to a gin and tonic.
